title: Client Instance Assertion — Self-Acting (client_credentials)
description: An AI agent workload authenticates itself using a Client Instance Assertion JWT, receiving a DPoP-bound access token where the instance is the principal — no user delegation, no act claim. This is the 'self-acting' case.

steps.label: Request Instance Assertion
steps.explanation: The client instance — a running container in Kubernetes — requests a Client Instance Assertion from its Instance Issuer. The issuer authenticates the runtime via platform attestation (e.g., Kubernetes service account token, SPIFFE SVID) and verifies the runtime is authorized to claim the given client_id.
annotations: §7.1: The issuer MUST authenticate the runtime before minting. Common pattern: thin OAuth adapter wrapping a SPIFFE JWT-SVID.
§7.1: The issuer MUST refuse to mint an assertion for a client_id the runtime is not authorized to claim.
The request names the target AS as audience — this prevents cross-AS replay of the resulting assertion.
Client metadata (instance_issuers array) is what authorizes this issuer to attest instances of this client.

steps.label: Instance Assertion JWT
1. JOSE Header
steps.value:
{
  "alg": "ES256",
  "kid": "4vC8agycHu6rnkEEJYAH6VuCe4JoSkPV",
  "typ": "client-instance+jwt"
}

§7.3: typ MUST be 'client-instance+jwt'.
The AS MUST reject assertions with any other typ value.
This prevents JWT confusion attacks where a WIMSE credential
or OAuth access token is mistaken for an instance assertion.

§7.3: MUST be signed with an asymmetric algorithm.
none and symmetric algorithms (HS256 etc.) MUST NOT be used.

steps.label: 2. JWT Payload
steps.value:
{
  "iss": "https://workload.openai.example.com",
  "sub": "spiffe://openai.example.com/codex/session-abc",
  "aud": "https://as.example.com",
  "client_id": "https://openai.example.com/codex",
  "iat": 1770000000,
  "nbf": 1770000000,
  "exp": 1770000300,
  "jti": "1a2b3c4d-5e6f",
  "cnf": { "jkt": "0ZcOCORZNYyOWyYiguA4I" },
  "sub_profile": "client_instance"
}

iss  — MUST match an instance_issuers descriptor in client metadata
sub  — the instance identifier (SPIFFE ID for this workload)
aud  — MUST name this specific AS (scopes assertion to one AS)
client_id — binds assertion to exactly one OAuth client (REQUIRED)
cnf.jkt — JWK SHA-256 thumbprint (RFC 9449 §3.1) of the instance's public key
jti  — unique ID; the AS tracks (iss, jti) pairs to prevent replay
exp  — §7.2 issuers SHOULD set ≤5 minutes from iat (300s here)
sub_profile — RECOMMENDED (elevated from OPTIONAL); value 'client_instance'

MUST NOT contain an act claim (§7.2 — AS rejects with invalid_grant if present)

steps.label: 3. Sign with ES256
steps.value:
ES256(
  PrivateKey(kid=4vC8agycHu6rnkEEJYAH6VuCe4JoSkPV),
  Base64Url(Header) + '.' + Base64Url(Payload)
) → ECDSA Signature

The AS fetches verification keys from the matching
instance_issuers descriptor:
  jwks_uri: https://workload.openai.example.com/jwks.json

Client metadata (trust anchor):
{
  "client_id": "https://openai.example.com/codex",
  "token_endpoint_auth_method": "client_instance_jwt",
  "instance_issuers": [{
    "issuer": "https://workload.openai.example.com",
    "jwks_uri": "https://workload.openai.example.com/jwks.json",
    "subject_syntax": "spiffe",
    "trust_domain": "openai.example.com",
    "spiffe_id": "spiffe://openai.example.com/codex/*",
    "signing_alg_values_supported": ["ES256"]
  }]
}

steps.explanation: The Instance Issuer mints a short-lived Client Instance Assertion JWT (300 seconds). The typ header 'client-instance+jwt' prevents JWT confusion attacks. The cnf.jkt claim carries the JWK SHA-256 thumbprint (RFC 9449 §3.1) of the instance's public key — this seeds the sender-constraint binding that flows through every subsequent step.
annotations: §7.3: JWS typ MUST be 'client-instance+jwt'. The AS MUST reject assertions with any other typ.
§7.2: exp MUST be short — issuers SHOULD set ≤5 minutes. 300 seconds here.
§7.2: jti MUST be unique. The AS tracks (iss, jti) tuples for replay prevention; cache entries MUST be retained until exp plus clock skew.
§7.2: cnf.jkt is the SHA-256 JWK Thumbprint (RFC 9449 §3.1) of the instance's EC public key. This same key signs all DPoP proofs.
§7.2: An act claim MUST NOT appear in the assertion — the AS MUST reject with invalid_grant if one is present.

steps.label: POST /token (client_creds)

artifacts.signing_key_hint: Instance ES256 Private Key — SAME key referenced by cnf.jkt in assertion

steps.label: 1. DPoP Proof Header
steps.value:
{
  "typ": "dpop+jwt",
  "alg": "ES256",
  "jwk": {
    "kty": "EC",
    "x": "EC_public_key_x",
    "y": "EC_public_key_y",
    "crv": "P-256"
  }
}

§8.5: The jwk MUST be the public key whose SHA-256 JWK Thumbprint
matches cnf.jkt in the instance assertion.
The AS verifies this linkage to ensure the same party
that holds the instance key is presenting the assertion.

AS MUST reject if JWK thumbprint ≠ cnf.jkt (invalid_request).

steps.label: 2. DPoP Proof Payload
steps.value:
{
  "jti": "dpop-proof-001",
  "htm": "POST",
  "htu": "https://as.example.com/token",
  "iat": 1770000010
}

htm + htu bind this proof to one specific HTTP method + URL.
A captured DPoP proof cannot be replayed at a different endpoint.

steps.label: 3. Full Token Request (client_instance_jwt auth)
steps.value:
grant_type=client_credentials
&scope=repo.write
&client_id=https://openai.example.com/codex
&actor_token=<Client Instance Assertion JWT from step 2>
&actor_token_type=
  urn:ietf:params:oauth:token-type:client-instance-jwt

--- No client_assertion ---
§8.3.1: token_endpoint_auth_method = 'client_instance_jwt'
The actor_token serves DUAL purpose:
  1) Client authentication (no client_assertion needed)
  2) Instance identification (names the specific runtime)

Request MUST include: client_id + actor_token + actor_token_type
Request MUST NOT carry: client_assertion or other auth credentials

§4.1: actor_token + actor_token_type are permitted on
client_credentials (this spec extends beyond RFC 8693).

§8.5: AS MUST verify DPoP proof JWK thumbprint = cnf.jkt.
AS MUST NOT issue bearer token — sender-constraint is mandatory.

steps.explanation: The instance sends a client_credentials grant with its Client Instance Assertion as the actor_token. Using the client_instance_jwt auth method, no separate client_assertion is needed — the assertion doubles as client authentication. The DPoP header proves possession of the instance's private key, which must match the cnf.jkt in the assertion.
annotations: §8.3.1: With client_instance_jwt auth method, request MUST carry client_id + actor_token + actor_token_type. MUST NOT carry client_assertion.
§4.1: This spec extends actor_token/actor_token_type to client_credentials — this was not permitted in RFC 8693.
§8.5: DPoP proof is REQUIRED when assertion carries cnf. AS MUST verify sender-constraint and MUST NOT issue bearer token.
actor_token_type = urn:ietf:params:oauth:token-type:client-instance-jwt

steps.label: 200 OK + Access Token
1. AS Validation Steps (§8.2)
steps.value:
Processing for client_instance_jwt auth method (§8.3.2):
1. Resolve client metadata for https://openai.example.com/codex
2. Parse actor_token, read iss = 'https://workload.openai.example.com'
3. Find descriptor in instance_issuers where issuer = iss
4. Fetch JWKS from descriptor's jwks_uri
5. Verify ES256 signature on instance assertion
6. Validate typ = 'client-instance+jwt' (reject any other value)
7. Validate: aud = this AS, exp not expired, nbf passed
8. Validate: client_id in assertion = client_id in request ✓
9. Check (iss, jti) = ('workload...', '1a2b3c4d-5e6f') not seen before
   AS MUST retain replay cache until exp + clock skew
10. Verify DPoP proof; SHA-256 JWK thumbprint = cnf.jkt ✓
11. §8.6.1: grant_type=client_credentials → SELF-ACTING case
12. §8.5: Issue sender-constrained token — MUST NOT issue bearer

steps.label: 2. Access Token (Self-Acting Case, §8.6.3)
steps.value:
{
  "iss": "https://as.example.com",
  "aud": "https://api.example.com",
  "sub": "spiffe://openai.example.com/codex/session-abc",
  "sub_profile": "client_instance",
  "client_id": "https://openai.example.com/codex",
  "scope": "repo.write",
  "cnf": { "jkt": "0ZcOCORZNYyOWyYiguA4I" },
  "iat": 1770000020,
  "exp": 1770003600
}

SELF-ACTING CASE (§8.6.3):
• sub = actor_token's sub (instance SPIFFE ID is the principal)
• sub_profile = actor_token's sub_profile ('client_instance') — SHOULD include
• cnf = instance key binding per §8.5 (same jkt as assertion)
• act MUST be omitted — instance is not delegating from a user
• §8.6.3: instance issuer iss is NOT represented as a top-level claim
  (sub_issuer is not a defined claim in this spec)

steps.explanation: The AS issues a DPoP-bound access token in the self-acting case. The instance SPIFFE ID is the top-level sub — no act claim is present because the instance itself is the principal. The cnf carries the instance's key thumbprint (jkt), completing the key-binding chain. Per §8.6.3, the instance issuer's iss is NOT propagated into the access token.
annotations: §8.6.3: Self-acting case (client_credentials) — sub = actor_token's sub (instance identifier). act MUST be omitted.
§8.6.3: sub_profile = actor_token's sub_profile; 'client_instance' SHOULD be included. RS uses this to apply instance-aware policy.
§8.5: token_type='DPoP' — AS MUST NOT omit cnf when instance assertion carried it. Bearer issuance is prohibited.
§9.1: RS MUST treat sub as an instance identifier (not a human user) when sub_profile='client_instance'.
§12.3: AS stores (iss, jti) = ('https://workload.openai.example.com', '1a2b3c4d-5e6f') to block replay until exp + clock skew.

steps.label: GET /api/repos (DPoP-bound)

artifacts.signing_key_hint: Instance ES256 Private Key — same key in cnf.jkt throughout

steps.label: 1. DPoP Proof for Resource
steps.value:
Header:
{
  "typ": "dpop+jwt",
  "alg": "ES256",
  "jwk": { <instance's public key> }
}

Payload:
{
  "jti": "dpop-proof-002",
  "htm": "GET",
  "htu": "https://api.example.com/api/repos",
  "iat": 1770000500,
  "ath": "SHA-256(access_token_raw_bytes)"
}

ath (access token hash) — RFC 9449 §4.2:
Binds this DPoP proof to the specific access token.
Prevents proof reuse with a different (possibly stolen) token.

steps.label: 2. RS Validation Steps (§9.1)
steps.value:
Resource Server validates:
1. Verify access token signature (AS public key)
2. Check sub_profile = 'client_instance'
   → sub is a workload instance, not a user
   → apply instance-aware access policy (not user-scoped)
3. Extract cnf.jkt from access token
4. Verify DPoP proof signature using JWK in proof header
5. Confirm JWK thumbprint in DPoP header = cnf.jkt in token
6. Verify: htm=GET, htu matches this request URL
7. Verify: ath = SHA-256(raw access token)
8. Apply scope authorization: scope=repo.write ✓

RS MUST NOT rely on act.iss for proof of possession —
top-level cnf is the authoritative binding key.

Key continuity chain:
  assertion cnf.jkt
    == DPoP token request proof JWK thumbprint
      == access token cnf.jkt
        == resource DPoP proof JWK thumbprint
  All four reference the SAME instance private key.

steps.explanation: The instance calls the resource server with its DPoP-bound access token. A fresh DPoP proof is generated for this specific URL and method, binding the ath (access token hash) to prevent proof reuse. The resource server checks sub_profile='client_instance' to know this is a workload, not a human user.
annotations: §8.5 / §9: The RS validates that the DPoP proof JWK thumbprint matches cnf.jkt in the access token.
§9.1: RS MUST apply instance-aware policy when sub_profile='client_instance'. User-scoped resources (e.g. /users/me) should be denied.
RFC 9449: ath claim binds DPoP proof to the specific access token — a proof captured at the AS cannot be replayed at the RS.
Security guarantee: an attacker intercepting the access token cannot use it without also possessing the instance private key.

steps.label: 200 OK + Resource
steps.explanation: The resource server returns the requested data. The full key-binding chain is intact: the instance's private key was used to bind the Client Instance Assertion (via cnf.jkt), the access token (via DPoP at token endpoint), and the resource request (via DPoP at the RS). Only the holder of that private key could have made this request.
annotations: §9.1: The RS verified sub_profile='client_instance' and applied non-user-scoped authorization policy.
Full trust chain: instance private key → cnf.jkt in assertion → cnf in access token → DPoP proof at RS.
Token theft is defeated: access token without the instance private key = useless. The cnf binding enforces this.
§10.2: This pattern is fully compatible with DPoP (RFC 9449) and mTLS (RFC 8705) sender-constraint mechanisms.
